nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 小麥帶區 (西澳)
nodes.summary: 小麥帶區 (西澳)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括Australian bustard、短嘴黑凤头鹦鹉、鹡鸰扇尾鹟等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Australian bustard
nodes.summary: Australian bustard（Australian bustard，学名 Ardeotis australis），分类上属于鸨形目，并归入鸨科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括灌丛、稀树草原，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、猎捕压力。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: 鸨形目
nodes.summary: 鸨形目（Otidiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联26种鸟类，如大鸨、Arabian bustard、灰颈鹭鸨等26种鸟类。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: Southwest Australia
nodes.summary: Southwest Australia is a biogeographic region in Western Australia.

nodes.name: Busselton
nodes.summary: Busselton是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Australian bustard，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鸨科
nodes.summary: 鸨科（Otididae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联26种鸟类，如大鸨、Arabian bustard、灰颈鹭鸨等26种鸟类。

nodes.name: Swan Coastal Plain
nodes.summary: Swan Coastal Plain是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Australian bustard，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: This bustard will enter denser areas of vegetation after fire, and is observed on artificial cleared areas such as golf courses and farmland. The historical range in Southwest Australia included the dunes and alluvial flats of the Swan Coastal Plain, as far south as Busselton, and at the interior in what became the Wheatbelt

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: The greatest fidelity to a local region is reported in areas of high rainfall in the north and northwest of Australia. The habitat favoured by the species is open grasslands, perhaps with some trees, spinifex plains and low shrublands

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: thumb|Ardeotis australis in front of a Bushfires in Australia|bushfire This bird remains relatively common and widespread across most of northern Australia (see Atlas), but its range appears to have contracted in the southeast of the continent during the last century, perhaps due to hunting (now illegal except for indigenous Australians), feral predators such as pigs and foxes (Vulpes vulpes) and habitat destruction
